hyperedges.label: Seven Warehouse Tables Form the Decision Warehouse
Intelligence Rail: Implementations of the Advisor Interface

nodes.label: Backtest-First Sequencing
nodes.rationale: v2.0 spent three months building live machinery before asking whether the strategy works; v2.1 inverts this so the market can veto the project at week 7 before any infrastructure spend. Moves the GO/NO-GO gate from week 12 to week 7, converting a NO-GO into a cheap, fast answer (seven weeks of reusable engineering, zero infrastructure cost).

nodes.label: Determinism Invariant
nodes.rationale: Same candles + same configuration must always produce byte-identical decisions. Intelligence that cannot be switched off and compared against the deterministic baseline is not trustworthy; enforced in CI via golden replay fixtures and forbidding wall-clock time, randomness, dict-ordering and float nondeterminism in decision paths.

nodes.label: Decision Warehouse (The Moat)
nodes.rationale: Anyone can copy a rule set; nobody can copy a year of the per-candle decision record (what the system saw, did, and what happened next), reproducible via config_hash. This is why decision capture (M13) is architecturally mandatory and never the corner to cut — Stages 3-5 convert this asset into compounding advantage.

nodes.label: Advisor Interface (The Advisor Seam)
nodes.rationale: The only door intelligence may use: returns confidence in [0,1] and an optional veto; may reduce position size or veto a qualified setup, must never create, enlarge or re-qualify anything, must be side-effect-free and <50ms. Guarantees intelligence advises but never overrides the deterministic risk layer (M8/M11).

nodes.label: Stage 3: Bayesian Evidence Engine
nodes.rationale: Per-feature likelihood ratios LR_f = P(f|win)/P(f|loss) learned from the warehouse, combined in log-odds and squashed by sigmoid into a calibrated confidence score. Laplace-smoothed; features below minimum sample size shrink toward LR=1. Deployed offline -> shadow -> advisory; can only reduce size or veto.

nodes.label: Stage 4: Quantum-Inspired Hypothesis Engine
nodes.rationale: Honest framing rule: there is no quantum computer anywhere in the system. It is a classical Bayesian belief state (hidden-Markov-style update) over market regimes; quantum vocabulary (superposition, measurement) is internal intuition only. External descriptions must say 'Bayesian belief-state model over market regimes' — implying exotic technology would mislead investors, customers, or the founder.

nodes.label: Stage 5: ResearchAgent (Autonomous Research Layer)
nodes.rationale: Mines the warehouse for candidate refinements and proposes — never deploys. Credentials can write only to proposals/ and read the warehouse; cannot write config/, DB config tables, or production. The human gate is enforced by permissions, not policy, and proven by a failing write test.

nodes.label: Eight Entry Gates
nodes.rationale: Evaluated sequentially; first failure rejects the setup and records the reason — short-circuit with recorded rejection reason is what makes rejections usable training data for Stage 3.

nodes.label: Replay Harness
nodes.rationale: One code path, two clocks: feeds historical candles through the identical per-candle sequence the live system uses. Fill simulation is deliberately pessimistic (spread, ATR-scaled slippage doubled in EXTREME state, commissions/swap, gap-through-stop fills at gap open) — a thin edge that costs erase is not an edge.

nodes.label: Walk-Forward Validation
nodes.rationale: A single backtest over one window with one parameter set is the classic overfitting trap; v2.1 replaces it with rolling 18-month train / 6-month test windows rolled forward 3 months across a 3-year corpus and >=3 instruments, with the verdict rendered on concatenated out-of-sample segments only, plus a +/-20% parameter-sensitivity sweep (an edge that exists only at one magic number is noise).

nodes.label: Golden Fixtures (Determinism in CI)
nodes.rationale: Curated candle sets with committed expected outputs (decision log, state transitions, signal rows); CI replays each fixture and fails on any byte difference. Intentional behaviour changes must regenerate fixtures in a reviewed commit — deliberately creating an audit moment.

nodes.label: Gate G3: GO/NO-GO at Week 7
nodes.rationale: Needs only strategy logic, historical data, and a replay harness with honest cost modelling — not a broker, container stack, or server. A NO-GO means stop and rethink, not soften a threshold until the verdict flips; the founder, not Claude Code, renders the decision by editing PHASE_STATUS.md.

nodes.label: 8-Week Demo Forward Test (Non-Compressible)
nodes.rationale: Fixed at 8 weeks minimum because fewer weeks means fewer market regimes sampled and a result that cannot be trusted; listed among the three things that are 'evidence, not engineering' and cannot be compressed.

nodes.label: ADR-001 Broker Bridge Decision (MT5 Native Windows)
nodes.rationale: Decided week 1, built only after a GO (Phase 4). Native Windows MT5 + official Python package recommended as most reliable; Wine-on-Linux rejected as fragile under terminal updates; EA socket bridge kept as fallback if Windows licensing becomes a constraint. Until Phase 4, M9 talks to the replay harness's simulated broker behind the same interface.
nodes.norm_label: adr-001 broker bridge decision (mt5 native windows)

nodes.label: Graphify Knowledge Layer
nodes.rationale: Turns spec, schema and code into one queryable typed graph committed in graphify-out/ and shared by every Claude Code session and hire. Code parsed locally via tree-sitter, no LLM calls, no source leaving the machine. Honest caveat: it accelerates the build but contributes nothing to the trading edge; the graph is derived state — spec and code remain the source of truth. Verified at v0.8.36, PyPI package 'graphifyy' (double y).

nodes.label: config_hash (Reproducibility Stamp)
nodes.rationale: sha256 of canonicalised parameters stamped on every warehouse row so any historical decision is reconstructable years later; applying a research proposal requires a human-signed config change that mints a new config_hash, making the audit trail automatic.

nodes.label: R-multiple (The One Normalised Number)
nodes.rationale: Trade outcome expressed in initial-risk units — the single field every learning loop trains on; the whole intelligence programme hinges on it being recorded correctly for every close, forever.

nodes.label: market_snapshot Table (Observation Spine)
nodes.rationale: One row per evaluated candle per instrument including NO_SETUP candles, because rejections are exactly the data that teaches Stage 3 what a near-miss looks like; partition-ready on ts but partitioning deferred until volume demands it.

nodes.label: trade_outcome Table (Append-Only)
nodes.rationale: Append-only; corrections supersede via the supersedes self-reference, never edit — preserves the audit trail of outcomes the learning loop trains on.

nodes.label: hypothesis_snapshot Table
nodes.rationale: Stays empty until Stage 4 but exists from day one — no migration later.

nodes.label: Claude Code Local Permission Allowlist (graphify tooling)
nodes.norm_label: claude code local permission allowlist (graphify tooling)
